overall_summary: PJM reports progress in its generator interconnection reforms, covering metrics across both Transition Cycles (Cycle 1 closing at 14.77 GW, Cycle 2 at 30.5 GW in Phase II), 51 RRI projects (11.8 GW), 42.5 GW of projects with signed service agreements, and a reversal of the three-year downward trend in new capacity additions in the 2026/2027 auction.

quotes.text: From July 2023 through December 2024, during the first 18 months of the transition period, PJM processed approximately 140 gigawatts ("GW") of generator interconnection requests.
quotes.concern: Successfully processed 140 GW of requests in 18 months

quotes.text: and on May 2, 2025, PJM communicated the results of the RRI process to Members: PJM selected 51 projects, including both uprates of existing facilities and new projects, and representing approximately 11.8 GW of energy and 9.3 GW of unforced Capacity ("UCAP").
quotes.concern: RRI selected 51 projects totaling 11.8 GW energy and 9.3 GW UCAP

quotes.text: Transition Cycle No. 1, which is in its Final Agreement Negotiation Phase, will be completed and generators in that Cycle will have signed service agreements to connect to the grid in January or February 2026, as PJM issued 135 Transition Cycle No. 1 service agreements for execution on December 10, 2025.
quotes.concern: Transition Cycle 1 completed with 135 service agreements signed by December 2025

quotes.text: At present, Transition Cycle No. 1 represents approximately 14.77 GW.

In addition to the projects currently under study or in service agreement negotiations, there are approximately 42.5 GW (installed capacity) of generation projects that have signed service agreements to connect to the grid. These projects have cleared PJM's interconnection process and have no PJM-associated impediment to construction.
quotes.concern: 42.5 GW of projects with signed agreements cleared interconnection with no impediments

quotes.text: Further, PJM notes that the total amount of new generation and generation uprates has increased to 2,669 MW of UCAP for the 2026/2027 Base Residual Auction ("BRA"), which reverses the downward trend in the amount of new generation and generation uprates of the three prior BRAs.
quotes.concern: New generation capacity increased to 2,669 MW UCAP in 2026/2027 auction, reversing downward trend

quotes.text: The RRI projects were added to Transition Cycle No. 2, which reached Phase II of the process on December 3, 2025, and is expected to be complete by February 2027.  At present, Transition Cycle No. 2 represents approximately 30.5 GW.
quotes.concern: Transition Cycle 2 reached Phase II in December 2025 and represents 30.5 GW, expected complete February 2027

bins.name: Interconnection reform progress metrics
bins.description: PJM reports progress across its interconnection reform initiatives: 140 GW of requests processed during the 18-month transition, 51 RRI projects totaling 11.8 GW selected, Transition Cycle 1 closing with 135 service agreements (14.77 GW) and Transition Cycle 2 at Phase II representing 30.5 GW (expected complete February 2027), 42.5 GW of projects with signed agreements cleared, and capacity additions in the 2026/2027 auction rising to 2,669 MW UCAP, reversing three prior auction declines.
